guideposts.label: Welcome to the live coverage of stage 15 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: A final mountain showdown ahead of the second rest day. After Tadej Pogacar’s victory at Le Markstein, the peloton face the first HC finish of the Tour de France 2026, at Plateau de Solaison, after 183.9 km on demanding roads. The peloton are set to roll from Champagnole at 13:10, to reach km 0 at 13:20. The winners will be crowned at around 17:45.

guideposts.label: Champagnole: Maes, Johannessens, Ghekiere
guideposts.detail: Known as a “Pearl of the Jura”, on the bank of the river Ain, Champagnole was a Tour stage town for the first time in 1937. Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on

guideposts.label: White jersey
guideposts.detail: Paul Seixas (19 years 9 months, 24 days) is the youngest rider to ever claim the white jersey, beating the record of Eric Vanderaerden (21 years 4 months 20 days after the prologue of 1983). Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) is now second in the standings, with a gap of just 3 seconds, while Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG) is third with a gap of 31’’.

guideposts.label: Team classification
guideposts.detail: For the 50th time in their Tour history, Lidl-Trek lead the overall team standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 7’26’’. Visma-Lease a Bike are back in third position overall (+42’32’’).

guideposts.label: Stage 15 of the Tour de France 2026 is ON!
guideposts.detail: The 169 riders just passed km 0.

guideposts.label: Lidl-Trek to flip the script?
guideposts.detail: Toms Skujins (Lidl-Trek) attacks at km 10. Emiel Verstrynge (Alpecin-Premier Tech) reacts to close the small gap.

guideposts.label: The peloton
The Paret-Peintres are close to home, so are the Swiss riders
guideposts.detail: Wearing the polka-dot jersey he’s been targeting for this Tour de France, Valentin Paret-Peintre will enjoy the iconic jersey on home roads with the final 76 kilometres set in his home department, Hautre-Savoie. Valentin and his brother Aurélien (racing with Decathlon CMA CGM) hail from Annemasse, less than 20 kilometres north of Col de la Croisette. The entire family is involved in the local cycl

guideposts.label: Intermediate sprint - km 17.3
The battle for the break is on
guideposts.detail: The likes of Bruno Armirail, Julius van den Berg, Olav Kooij and Kasper Asgreen have all tried to get away. Meanwhile, Isaac del Toro suffers a mechanical.

guideposts.label: Barguil sets off, Voisard joins in
guideposts.detail: Einer Rubio and Nicolas Vinokurov also try to make the break.

guideposts.label: Alaphilippe on the move at km 27
guideposts.detail: Degenkolb follows the French man. Delbove and Turgis close the gap with Mohoric.

guideposts.label: Four riders have pulled a few metres clear of the peloton
The first climb of the day: Côte des Rousses
guideposts.detail: The attackers of Total Energies can draw inspiration from Lilian Calmejane's victory at Station des Rousses on 8 July 2017, which made for a very special birthday gift to their team president Jean-René Bernaudeau. The race came from a different side.

guideposts.label: Netcompany Ineos up the ante
guideposts.detail: The attackers are reeled in a bit more than 5 kilometres from the summit. Josh Tarling accelerates with Thymen Arensman on his wheel.

guideposts.label: The four riders at the front have been caught
Mechanical for Seixas
guideposts.detail: The Frenchman needs a new rearwheel.

guideposts.label: Summit (cat 3) - 1098m
Paul Seixas has rejoined the peloton
Arensman and Azparren on the move
guideposts.detail: They've opened a gap after the summit.

guideposts.label: 41.4 kilometres in the first hour
guideposts.detail: Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos) and Xabier Mikel Azparren (Pinarello Q36.5) still lead the way. Magnus Cort, Anders Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) and Robbe Dhondt (Picnic PostNL) trail by 10 seconds. The peloton are right behind.

guideposts.label: Nelson Oliveira has managed to break away from the peloton
50 kilometres of battle
guideposts.detail: Arensman and Azparren still lead the way. Oliveira is closing the gap on them. A small chase group has emerged with Matthews, Schmid, A. Johannessen, Van Dijke, Garcia Pierna...

guideposts.label: Thymen Arensman and Xabier Azparren are caught by a group of eight chasers
The battle goes on
guideposts.detail: Tim van Dijke (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos), Mauro Schmid, Michael Matthews (Jayco AlUla), Tobias Johannessen, Anders Skaarseth (Uno-X Mobility), Raul García Pierna, Nelson Oliveira (Movistar Team), Ion Izagirre (Cofidis), and Xabier Mikel Azparren (Pinarello-Q36.5) are only 10 seconds ahead of the bunch at km 60.

guideposts.label: 4 stage winners in the breakaway
guideposts.detail: Australia’s Michael Matthews (Jayco AlUla) has the most stage wins to his name, with four to his credit: Revel 2016, Rodez 2017, Romans-sur-Isère 2017 and Mende 2022. The experienced Ion Izagirre (Cofidis) first won ten years ago in Morzine, then in 2023 in Belleville-en-Beaujolais. The Dutchman Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos) triumphed at Luchon-Superbagnères exactly one year ago to the day, b

guideposts.label: EF Education-Easypost are continuing their team time trial to catch up with the breakaway
EF Education-EasyPost catch the attackers
guideposts.detail: Richard Carapaz and his teammates pushed hard on an uphill section 77 kilometres into the stage.

guideposts.label: Vinokurov opens a gap
guideposts.detail: The Kazakh attacker will soon face the final 100 kilometres of the stage.

guideposts.label: Memories from Valserhône and Bellegarde-sur-Valserine
guideposts.detail: Valserhône was born in 2019, merging the towns of Bellegarde-sur-Valserine, Châtillon-en-Michaille and Lancrans. The former saw Thomas Voeckler win stage 10 of the Tour de France 2012 after 194.5 km of racing from Mâcon, with the Col du Grand Colombier as the main climbing challenge of the day. Bellegarde-sur-Valserine has also hosted stages of the Tour de l’Avenir as well as the Tour de l’Ain and

guideposts.label: A group is trying to break away at the front
The average speed after 2 hours: 48.1 km/h
guideposts.detail: The attackers covered 54.8 km in the second hour to get away. The gap to the bunch is up to 2'20''.

guideposts.label: Wellens and Hagenes work together
guideposts.detail: The Belgian all-rounder struggled at the start of the day, but he's moved to the front of the bunch after the break got away. Per Strand Hagenes (Visma-Lease a Bike) takes turn with him to control the gap at around 2'30''.

guideposts.label: A most special day for Seixas
guideposts.detail: Paul Seixas and his many fans were struck and delighted at the end of stage 14, as the wonderkid battled with Jonas Vingegaard and Isaac del Toro on the Vosges. Taking a second top-3 finish, France’s new hero also claimed the white jersey as leader of the young riders’ standings. He already made history as the youngest participant since 1937. He is now the youngest rider to ever wear the yellow, g

guideposts.label: 70 kilometres to go
guideposts.detail: The gap is up to 2'45''. In the virtual standings, Pidcock moves up to 4th place, in between Remco Evenepoel (+5'04'') and Paul Seixas (+5'19'').

guideposts.label: There’s a lack of harmony within the breakaway
guideposts.detail: A radio communications from Bahrain Victorious was just iared on TV, suggesting riders at the front are not willing to collaborate with Tom Pidcock. The gap to the bunch is down to 2'25''. Nicolas Vinokurov (XDS Astana) attacks from the breakaway.

guideposts.label: Van Moer pulls the break
guideposts.detail: The gap increases again: 2'45''. The attackers are 62 kilomtres from the finish.

guideposts.label: Soon on the slopes of Le Salève - Col de la Croisette
guideposts.detail: The riders face a brutal side today: 4.7 km at 11.2%! Brent Van Moer (Pinarello Q36.5) is emptying his tank ahead of the ascent. The gap is up to 2'55'' (the max so far today).

guideposts.label: Pidcock pulls the break
guideposts.detail: On his wheel: Simmons, Van Wilder, Voisard, Schmid, Rubio, Frigo...

guideposts.label: The break explodes
guideposts.detail: Pidcock leads the way. Only Simmons, Van Wilder, Voisard, Schmid, Costiou, Rubio, and Yates follow him. In the peloton, Isaac del Toro is at the back. Piganzoli is now setting the pace.

guideposts.label: Into the final 50 kilometres
guideposts.detail: Van Wilder and Schmid are distanced. Simmons accelerates.

guideposts.label: Quinn Simmons attacks at the front of the race
Summit (cat 1) - 1173m
Simmons leads the way to the summit
guideposts.detail: Pidcock, Voisard, Yates and Rubio are 5 seconds behind at the summit.

guideposts.label: Results at Le Salève - Col de la Croisette (cat 1, km 136)
guideposts.detail: 1. Simmons, 10 pts 2. Pidcock, 8 pts 3. Voisard, 6 pts 4. Yates, 4 pts 5. Rubio, 2 pts 6. Costiou, 1 pt

guideposts.label: The 5 leaders get back together; Jorgenson pulls the GC group
guideposts.detail: The gap at the summit: 3'10''.

guideposts.label: 38.5 km covered in the third hour
guideposts.detail: The average speed so far: 44.9 km/h.

guideposts.label: 6 riders in the lead
guideposts.detail: Ewen Costiou (Groupama-FDJ United) closed the gap inside the final kilometre of ascent. Mauro Schmid (Jayco AlUla) is just behind.

guideposts.label: Ewen Costiou has managed to catch up with the leading group
Summit (cat 3) - 820m
The situation at the summit
guideposts.detail: Four chasers are 45'' behind the leaders: Kevin Vauquelin, Alex Baudin, Ilan Van Wilder, and Aurélien Paret-Peintre. Guillaume Martin-Guyonnet is alone with a gap of 1'45''. The gap to the bunch remains at 2'45''.

guideposts.label: La Roche-sur-Foron (km 158.7): from Mottet to Kwiatkowski
guideposts.detail: Home a stage start of the Critérium du Dauphiné in 1988, La Roche-sur-Foron cheered for Charly Mottet, overall leader of the race, before the peloton headed to Chambéry, where Acacio Da Silva won the stage ahead of Mariano Sanchez and Charly Mottet, who retained the leader’s jersey. Cycling enthusiasts mostly associate La Roche-sur-Foron with Michael Kwiatkoswki’s success in the Tour de France 202

guideposts.label: The peloton is closing in on the breakaway riders
Vingegaard and Del Toro have crashed
guideposts.detail: The Mexican is rapidly on his feet. The Dane is in pain.

guideposts.label: Jonas Vingegaard gets into the ambulance
guideposts.detail: The race doctors put his right arm in a sling.

guideposts.label: Vingegaard has abandoned the race
guideposts.detail: He has finished his previous five participations on the podium.

guideposts.label: Plateau de Solaison, a recent pearl
guideposts.detail: A recent discovery in modern cycling, the Plateau de Solaison is a jewel that immediately left a strong impression with its demanding ascent and its spectacular views. Tour des Pays de Savoie (in 2012) and Tour de l’Avenir (2014) first ventured there before the Critérium du Dauphiné, now Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, concluded on these slopes in 2017, 2022, and 2026. When the ascent first appeared in

guideposts.label: The 47th HC climb of the Tour
guideposts.detail: With its impressive figures - 11.3 km of ascent with an average gradient of 9% -, the climb to Plateau de Solaison makes its debut in great fashion on the roads of the Tour de France. It’s the 47th climb to be classified HC since the introduction of this category in 1979. That year, only Col du Galibier and L’Alpe d’Huez were HC. The last climb to join the list of HC ascents was Col de Vars, in 20

guideposts.label: Yates gets back to the Pidcock group
guideposts.detail: THat makes four chasers 20 seconds behind Schmid.

guideposts.label: Quinn Simmons has launched an attack and is closing in on the Swiss rider
Simmons is caught
guideposts.detail: Yates sets the pace, followed by Pogacar and Del Toro.

guideposts.label: Into the final 3 km
guideposts.detail: +33'' Seixas, Lipowitz +55'' Yates +1' Simmons, Ayuso

guideposts.label: Isaac Del Toro attacks with one km to go
Del Toro attacks a couple of times...
guideposts.detail: Evenepoel counters!

guideposts.label: Remco Evenepoel wins stage 15 of the Tour de France 2026!
guideposts.detail: Pogacar finishes 2nd, Del Toro loses a handful of seconds.

guideposts.label: Top 5
guideposts.detail: 1. Remco Evenepoel (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe) 2. Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) 3. Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates XRG), +6'' 4. Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), +58'' 5. Florian Lipowitz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe)
